Interview.title: Technical interview
Interview.instructionsHtml:
⚠️ Turn on transcription!

Intro

  • 1h-long, multiple sections

  • No coding, this will be during the trial: more Q&A and architecture

Q&A

  • We don’t expect that you’re strong in all areas. It’s just to map your knowledge. It’s absolutely normal to not have all the answers.

  • Try to answer as succinctly as you can, I’ll probe if needed. Communication matters.

Frontend

What is the difference between SSR, CSR, and static generation? Tradeoffs?

What causes a React component to re-render? How do you prevent unnecessary renders?

How do you handle global state in a frontend app? When is context not enough?

How do you debug a slow UI?

Web

In the HTTP Protocol, there are GET and POST requests. What are the differences between the 2?

What different forms of caching could you use for a high-traffic website CDN, In-memory cache, request

What is a cookie? How are cookie set?

How should you store passwords in a database?

Javascript

What is the event loop and why does it matter for UI performance?

What are common causes of memory leaks in frontend apps?

How do you type API responses safely?

How do you structure async data fetching in a React app?

DBs

What does it mean for a relational DB to be normalized? What downsides? (slow with joins) What’s good? (consistency, space, write performance)

What are database indexes? What are they for? What downsides? (write slower, take space) What’s good? (faster read)

How are indexes implemented in the DB? What’s going on under the hood?

Have you heard of compound indexes? (multi-column indexes?)

How can you determine how a DB will execute a particular query? Suppose a query is slow, you want to see which indices the DB will use.

Algorithms

Can you describe how hashmaps are implemented?

Is it possible to map two objects to the same bucket? What happens then?

You create a hashmap with 64 buckets, and then add 64k objects to it? What happens?

System design

You have a backend that returns cars for sale with attributes (make, model, price, location, mileage, color). The backend exposes a basic endpoint like GET /cars that supports filtering, sorting, and pagination.

Build a frontend that lets users:

  • filter by multiple attributes (including ranges like mileage and price)

  • combine filters (AND, optional OR for some fields)

  • sort results

  • paginate or infinite scroll

  • share a search via URL

Followups

  • Backend is slow and responses can arrive out of order. How do you avoid showing stale results?

  • Dataset is huge. How do you keep the UI fast?

  • How do you persist searches and restore on refresh?

Look for

  • URL as source of truth (deep links)

  • handling loading, error, empty states

  • debouncing, cancellation, race conditions

  • caching and stale results

  • rendering performance for large lists (virtualization)

Product discussion

Nango

Merge

Additional Notes

Conclusion

  • Do you have any question?

  • Sync with Robin, if all good, we can do a SuperDay

  • Thanks a lot

 - combine filters (AND, optional OR for some fields)

 - sort results

 - paginate or infinite scroll

 - share a search via URL

Followups

 - Backend is slow and responses can arrive out of order. How do you avoid showing stale results?

 - How do you persist searches and restore on refresh?

Look for

 - URL as source of truth (deep links)

 - handling loading, error, empty states

 - debouncing, cancellation, race conditions

MERGE

  • filter by multiple attributes (including ranges like mileage and price)

  • combine filters (AND, optional OR for some fields)

  • sort results

  • paginate or infinite scroll

  • share a search via URL

Followups

  • Backend is slow and responses can arrive out of order. How do you avoid showing stale results?

  • Dataset is huge. How do you keep the UI fast?

  • How do you persist searches and restore on refresh?

Look for

  • URL as source of truth (deep links)

  • handling loading, error, empty states

  • debouncing, cancellation, race conditions

  • caching and stale results

  • rendering performance for large lists (virtualization)

Product discussion

Nango

Merge

Additional Notes

Conclusion

results.title: Technical interview
results.instructionsHtml:
⚠️ Turn on transcription!

Intro

  • 1h-long, multiple sections

  • No coding, this will be during the trial: more Q&A and architecture

Q&A

  • We don’t expect that you’re strong in all areas. It’s just to map your knowledge. It’s absolutely normal to not have all the answers.

  • Try to answer as succinctly as you can, I’ll probe if needed. Communication matters.

Frontend

What is the difference between SSR, CSR, and static generation? Tradeoffs?

What causes a React component to re-render? How do you prevent unnecessary renders?

How do you handle global state in a frontend app? When is context not enough?

How do you debug a slow UI?

Web

In the HTTP Protocol, there are GET and POST requests. What are the differences between the 2?

What different forms of caching could you use for a high-traffic website CDN, In-memory cache, request

What is a cookie? How are cookie set?

How should you store passwords in a database?

Javascript

What is the event loop and why does it matter for UI performance?

What are common causes of memory leaks in frontend apps?

How do you type API responses safely?

How do you structure async data fetching in a React app?

DBs

What does it mean for a relational DB to be normalized? What downsides? (slow with joins) What’s good? (consistency, space, write performance)

What are database indexes? What are they for? What downsides? (write slower, take space) What’s good? (faster read)

How are indexes implemented in the DB? What’s going on under the hood?

Have you heard of compound indexes? (multi-column indexes?)

How can you determine how a DB will execute a particular query? Suppose a query is slow, you want to see which indices the DB will use.

Algorithms

Can you describe how hashmaps are implemented?

Is it possible to map two objects to the same bucket? What happens then?

You create a hashmap with 64 buckets, and then add 64k objects to it? What happens?

System design

You have a backend that returns cars for sale with attributes (make, model, price, location, mileage, color). The backend exposes a basic endpoint like GET /cars that supports filtering, sorting, and pagination.

Build a frontend that lets users:

  • filter by multiple attributes (including ranges like mileage and price)

  • combine filters (AND, optional OR for some fields)

  • sort results

  • paginate or infinite scroll

  • share a search via URL

Followups

  • Backend is slow and responses can arrive out of order. How do you avoid showing stale results?

  • Dataset is huge. How do you keep the UI fast?

  • How do you persist searches and restore on refresh?

Look for

  • URL as source of truth (deep links)

  • handling loading, error, empty states

  • debouncing, cancellation, race conditions

  • caching and stale results

  • rendering performance for large lists (virtualization)

Product discussion

Nango

Merge

Additional Notes

Conclusion

  • Do you have any question?

  • Sync with Robin, if all good, we can do a SuperDay

  • Thanks a lot

Low level systems

In C, you have to use malloc and free. Can you talk about what these are and what they do Allocate heap in memory, return a pointer to the memory. Fetch pages of memory from the OS using system calls (mmap, sbrk)

  • Imagine I am your co-worker, I built a web-scraper, it scrapes Craigslist and Cars.com and download information on used cars

    • App server

    • You release the API, it gets popoular, your now getting millions of API requests per day. What design changes do you need to make to support this load?

      • Vertical scaling

      • Query optimization

      • Replicas, maybe sharding

WHAT DIFFERENT FORMS OF CACHING COULD YOU USE FOR A HIGH-TRAFFIC WEBSITE CDN, IN-MEMORY CACHE, REQUEST


WHAT IS A COOKIE? HOW ARE COOKIE SET?


HOW SHOULD YOU STORE PASSWORDS IN A DATABASE?


JAVASCRIPT


WHAT IS THE EVENT LOOP AND WHY DOES IT MATTER FOR UI PERFORMANCE?


WHAT ARE COMMON CAUSES OF MEMORY LEAKS IN FRONTEND APPS?


HOW DO YOU TYPE API RESPONSES SAFELY?


HOW DO YOU STRUCTURE ASYNC DATA FETCHING IN A REACT APP?


ALGORITHMS


CAN YOU DESCRIBE HOW HASHMAPS ARE IMPLEMENTED?


IS IT POSSIBLE TO MAP TWO OBJECTS TO THE SAME BUCKET? WHAT HAPPENS THEN?


YOU CREATE A HASHMAP WITH 64 BUCKETS, AND THEN ADD 64K OBJECTS TO IT? WHAT HAPPENS?





SYSTEM DESIGN




You have a backend that returns cars for sale with attributes (make, model, price, location, mileage, color). The backend exposes a basic endpoint like GET /cars that supports filtering, sorting, and pagination.

Build a frontend that lets users:

 - filter by multiple attributes (including ranges like mileage and price)

 - combine filters (AND, optional OR for some fields)

 - sort results

 - paginate or infinite scroll

 - share a search via URL

Followups

 - Backend is slow and responses can arrive out of order. How do you avoid showing stale results?

 - Dataset is huge. How do you keep the UI fast?

 - How do you persist searches and restore on refresh?

Look for

 - URL as source of truth (deep links)

 - handling loading, error, empty states

 - debouncing, cancellation, race conditions

 - caching and stale results

 - rendering performance for large lists (virtualization)


PRODUCT DISCUSSION


NANGO


MERGE


ADDITIONAL NOTES


CONCLUSION

 - Do you have any question?

 - Sync with Robin, if all good, we can do a SuperDay

 - Thanks a lot

Intro

  • 1h-long, multiple sections

  • No coding, this will be during the trial: more Q&A and architecture

Q&A

  • We don’t expect that you’re strong in all areas. It’s just to map your knowledge. It’s absolutely normal to not have all the answers.

  • Try to answer as succinctly as you can, I’ll probe if needed. Communication matters.

Frontend

What is the difference between SSR, CSR, and static generation? Tradeoffs?

What causes a React component to re-render? How do you prevent unnecessary renders?

How do you handle global state in a frontend app? When is context not enough?

How do you debug a slow UI?

Web

In the HTTP Protocol, there are GET and POST requests. What are the differences between the 2?

What different forms of caching could you use for a high-traffic website CDN, In-memory cache, request

What is a cookie? How are cookie set?

How should you store passwords in a database?

Javascript

What is the event loop and why does it matter for UI performance?

What are common causes of memory leaks in frontend apps?

How do you type API responses safely?

How do you structure async data fetching in a React app?

Algorithms

Can you describe how hashmaps are implemented?

Is it possible to map two objects to the same bucket? What happens then?

You create a hashmap with 64 buckets, and then add 64k objects to it? What happens?
